title: lathyrus montanus var. angustissimus
intro: Lathyrus montanus var. angustissimus is a variety of the annual wild pea, specifically a narrow-leafed form of the plant, native to various parts of the world. It contains lathyrine and ODAP (oxalyldiaminopropionic acid), which have been found to have medicinal properties, particularly as a potential treatment for certain types of cancer and neurological disorders. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as the potential to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. In traditional medicine, lathyrus montanus var. angustissimus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
